items.title: GOES-19 IR: torrential rain over the Hill Country
items.caption: GOES-19 infrared view of the slow-moving storm complex that dropped 12+ inches on the Hill Country in 48 hours (regional frame; coords nominal center).

items.title: Sunrise satellite view, peak flood morning
items.caption: Sunrise GOES imagery on the morning the Guadalupe rose 32 ft in 4 hours at Center Point (time approximate; regional frame).

items.title: FM 481 bridge collapse over the Nueces River
items.credit: Texas DPS South Texas Region, Aircraft Operations Division
items.license: Official government public-safety release; link only
items.caption: DPS aerial shows a segment of the FM 481 (Old Eagle Pass Road) bridge gone, the remaining span nearly submerged by the record Nueces; collapse reported Friday morning Jul 17 (coords approx: FM 481 Nueces crossing ~10 mi SW of Uvalde).

items.title: Historic Arcadia Loop Bridge destroyed, Goat Creek, Kerrville
items.caption: Goat Creek floodwaters tore a 15-20 ft hole through the historic Arcadia Loop Bridge and severed a city water main; one of 6 Kerr County bridges with major damage (time and coords approximate).

items.title: US 57 underwater at the Nueces River, La Pryor
items.credit: Zavala County Sheriff's Office via Texas Tribune
items.caption: Early-morning Jul 16 view of US 57 crossing the Nueces at La Pryor after a historic 20-ft rise; TxDOT closed 50 miles of US 57 from La Pryor to I-35 (time and coords approximate).

items.title: Barge strikes Sidney Baker Bridge, Kerrville
items.caption: A flood-loosed barge struck the Sidney Baker (SH 16) bridge over the Guadalupe; bridge closed pending structural assessment (time and coords approximate).
